summary: Do not ship the plan yet. P-1 and the committed RED witness are sound, but P-2 and P-3 remain unresolved blockers. No new critical issue or materially simpler safe fix was found; naive rechecks and coarse locks retain TOCTOU or change blocking behavior.

findings.title: P-2 remains: an overlapping failed put still changes GC behavior
findings.body: The contract restores X whenever a pin is live at grave time, before that put's outcome is known, yet immediately claims every failed, cancelled, ENOSPC, or rename-failed put is reaped identically to today. A put can observe X, remain live while GC settles and restores it (`gc_deleted == 0`), then fail its pointer rename with no pointer created. Repeated failures overlapping each pass can therefore defer reclamation indefinitely. T-C1 misses this because it runs reconciliation only after the failing put has returned and its live pin has dropped. This is the same uncharacterized capacity/statistics flip P-2 identified.
findings.recommendation: Do not treat `live` as a successful outcome. Track terminal outcomes for pins present at grave time and restore only after a landed commit, or route the reclamation change to another pipeline. Add a deterministic test that parks a live put through `settle`, then forces its rename to fail and requires `gc_deleted == 1`.

findings.title: P-3 remains: RenameReceipt does not enforce the claimed ordering
findings.body: The plan claims a pre-grave `protected()` check cannot compile, but `RenameReceipt` is an unbound unit token and the generic `pub(crate)` `rename_durable` can produce one for arbitrary paths. Code in `pins.rs` can therefore obtain a receipt from an unrelated rename and perform the unsafe check before the blob-to-grave transition without changing `atomic.rs`. T-B2 would remain green because its put is already live before `pre_grave` and fully landed before GC resumes, so any such precheck sees either `live` or `landed`. The load-bearing check-to-rename mutant is still unpinned.
findings.recommendation: Bind and consume the proof for the exact SHA and blob-to-grave transition, or evaluate protection through a callback that is reachable only after that rename. Revise T-B2 so the put begins or lands after a modeled precheck but before the grave rename, and require the unsafe mutant to fail.

next_steps: Revise the outcome protocol and add the overlapping eventual-failure witness for P-2.
Make the grave proof transition-specific and add a deterministic precheck-to-rename mutant witness for P-3.
Repeat the plan review without re-running machine-owned GREEN verification.
